body.title: Igor() boot smoke test — full pytest passed despite TypeError on instantiation
body.result: Smoke test shipped (commit 7086aca9). Subprocess invocation: spawns python -c 'Igor(instance_id=...)', forwards conftest's IGOR_HOME_SEARCH_PATH so Cortex writes to test schema not production. Asserts returncode==0 and 'BOOT_OK' in stdout. Bug-catching verified: temporarily reverted main.py:496 to broken Cortex(self.db_path, ...) form, confirmed test fails with TypeError visible in captured stderr; restored. Suite: 4307 → 4308 passing. ~12s wall.
body.description:
The Cortex constructor sweep (T-sqlite-out-wild-0001-db, commit 9bfc31ae) missed main.py:496 — Igor() raised TypeError on instantiation for an unknown number of days, but the full pytest suite passed clean. The Igor class has near-zero direct coverage; tests stub it (e.g. _IgorShape in test_igor_datacenter_boot.py) rather than instantiating it.

**Bug class:** anything that breaks Igor.__init__ ships silently because no test runs the constructor end-to-end. This is a coverage gap big enough to land a HIGH-inertia rename without anyone noticing.

**The smoke test we need:** one test that does `Igor(instance_id='wild-0001-test')` (or whatever isolation pattern works) and asserts the boot sequence completes without exception. It does NOT need to:
- Actually wire the datacenter (use injected None or stub client)
- Run cognition
- Hold long-lived state

It just needs to verify the constructor runs to completion, so future sweeps that rename a Cortex parameter or change a sub-component shape break this test instead of leaking to runtime.

**Affected files:** tests/test_igor_boot_smoke.py (NEW). Possibly conftest.py to add an isolation fixture if needed.

**Design rules:** test-coverage-for-load-bearing-classes, no-fragile-mocks (use real Cortex with isolated test schema, not deep MagicMock).

**Scope boundary:** IN — single boot smoke test that instantiates Igor and asserts no exception. OUT — full Igor lifecycle tests (run_turn, etc.); refactoring Igor.__init__ to be more testable; coverage of nested subsystems (those have their own tests).

**Test plan:** the test IS the deliverable. Acceptance: re-introducing the main.py:496 bug locally causes the new test to fail.

**Why now:** the bug we're fixing in T-flaky-tests would have been caught by this test had it existed. Filing as the immediate response to the gap surfaced by T-bystander-main-py-496-cortex-sweep-miss.
